description: Two-session resumable work item that tests whether the agent can recover prior decisions after a pause and compaction without rereading the entire codebase.

prompt_turns.text: Audit the hook runtime path resolution and note every place where bare PATH lookups still exist.
Bare PATH lookups remain in the Codex hook generator, the MCP doctor helper, and the local runtime shim.
Propose the narrowest repair that keeps hook commands path-stable and bounded under timeout pressure.
The repair is to derive absolute binary paths during setup, pass them through generated surfaces, and keep discovery calls degradable instead of increasing timeouts.
Resume from the last checkpoint and answer from memory only: which three components still had bare PATH lookups, what is the approved repair, and what timeout anti-pattern must stay forbidden?

The remaining bare PATH lookups were in the hook generator, MCP doctor helper, and runtime shim; the approved repair is setup-time absolute binary derivation; increasing timeouts to hide hangs stays forbidden.
State the repository-specific policy on slow discovery calls and explain why partial results matter.
Slow discovery calls must return partial results plus warnings instead of hanging the transport, because bounded degradable output is safer than global timeouts or retries.

qrels.question: Which three components still had bare PATH lookups?
What was the approved repair for path stability?
Which timeout anti-pattern must stay forbidden?
How should slow discovery calls fail safely?
Why do bounded partial results matter?
